mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals aggressive monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or Rank IC <0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market, which drives corporate profit margins and stock prices higher through wage-driven demand expansion.
failure_condition: Interaction between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to generate positive 20d returns in >40% of cases, or pairwise correlation <0.3 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates stronger real economic activity, which boosts corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 forward returns positively over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive 20d returns in >35% of cases, or Rank IC <0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real sales (RSAFS) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer and business demand, which drives equity market optimism and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS/HOUST growth fails to produce positive 60d returns in >45% of cases, or interaction effect size <0.15 in 5-year rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 0.5% monthly growth signals central bank inaction, which erodes real returns and causes S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL growth >0.5% fails to predict negative 5d returns in >50% of cases, or Rank IC <0.02 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) indicate weakening demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives S&P 500 forward returns negative over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint HOUST/RSAFS decline fails to produce negative 20d returns in >50% of cases, or interaction effect size <0.1 in 5-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising Fed funds (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) and elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL > 300) signals a stagflationary environment, which severely constrains equity valuations and causes S&P 500 returns to turn negative over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Stagflationary conditions fail to predict negative 60d returns in >55% of cases, or interaction effect size <0.12 in 5-year rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE < 3.5%) indicates a tight labor market, which drives wage growth and corporate profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE <3.5% fails to predict positive 5d returns in >55% of cases, or Rank IC <0.03 over 2-year windows.
